rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. THE ESTATE OF STANLEY JELKS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 20, 2020 line 7. PLAINTIFF JOHN STEWART COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment is GRANTED, subject to opposition. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FILLMORE STREET DEVELOPMENT GROUP, LLC. VS. ATIQUE REHMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Attendance Of Jeff Jurow At His Deposition With Document Production And Req. For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 20, 2020 line 8. DEFENDANT ATIQUE REHMAN Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Attendance Of Jeff Jurow At His Deposition With Document Production And Req. For Monetary Sanctions Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion to Compel Deposition of Jeff Jurrow. is GRANTED. Plaintiff's counsel's declaration is devoid of any explanation as to the failure to engage in meet and confer efforts. While Plaintiff objected to the deposition date selected by the Defendant, Plaintiff failed to respond to July 6, 2020 meet and confer letter requesting deposition dates; there was no response as of July 9, 2020, the date of Mr. Bravo's declaration; no response as of July 13, 2020, the date of the filing of the motion; and no response on July 15, 2020, the date the opposition was filed. Defendant generally appeared in this action as is admitted by defense counsel. CCP 2025.210. Jeff Jurrow to appear for deposition the week of July 27, 2020 on a mutually agreeable date. If parties cannot agree on a date, the deposition shall take place on July 29, 2020. Sanctions granted in the amount of $800. If Plaintiff appears for deposition in accordance with this order the part of the order granting sanctions shall be of no force and effect. If Plaintiff fails to appear, sanctions to be paid no later than August 14, 2020. =(501/CFH)
